Developer.description: Software Developers design, build, and maintain software applications, websites, and digital systems. They use coding languages and logic to solve technical problems and create tools that power modern life.
learning_path: Learn a fundamental programming language like Python or JavaScript.
Build small personal projects to practice coding principles.
Understand version control (Git) and command line basics.
Learn about data structures, algorithms, and databases.
Specialise in a domain (e.g., Web, Mobile, Cloud, AI).
Create a portfolio on GitHub and contribute to open-source projects.

Designer.description: Designers combine art, technology, and empathy to create visually appealing and functional layouts, products, and user experiences. They focus on how users interact with websites, apps, and physical media.
learning_path: Master industry-standard design tools like Figma or Adobe Illustrator.
Study visual design fundamentals: grids, layouts, typography, and color harmony.
Learn user-centric design principles and user research methodologies.
Redesign existing websites/apps to practice and critique usability.
Build a strong visual portfolio on platforms like Behance or Dribbble.
Develop soft skills in presenting designs and receiving constructive criticism.

Analyst.description: Analysts interpret complex datasets, identify patterns, and draw actionable insights to help businesses make smart decisions. They act as a bridge between raw data and business strategy.
learning_path: Learn advanced Excel functions and master writing SQL queries.
Learn a data-oriented programming language (Python - Pandas/NumPy, or R).
Study basic statistics, probability, and analytical frameworks.
Learn data visualization tools like Tableau or Power BI to build dashboards.
Practice analyzing public datasets (e.g., from Kaggle) and writing summary reports.
Gain domain knowledge in finance, marketing, or business operations.

Manager.description: Managers coordinate teams, set project strategies, manage resources, and oversee operations to achieve business goals. They excel in communication, organization, leadership, and decision-making.
learning_path: Understand project management methodologies (Agile, Scrum, Waterfall).
Develop core soft skills: conflict resolution, negotiation, and active listening.
Earn relevant certifications (e.g., Certified ScrumMaster, CAPM/PMP, or Prince2).
Practice organizing group events, club activities, or small team projects.
Learn how to define key performance indicators (KPIs) and track metrics.
Study corporate finance, business strategy, and product development lifecycles.

Engineer.description: Engineers apply physics, mathematics, and materials science to design, build, and test physical structures, machines, electrical systems, and hardware devices.
learning_path: Study advanced mathematics (calculus, linear algebra) and physics principles.
Learn a CAD software tool to create 3D models and structural drafts.
Participate in physical building projects (e.g., Arduino, 3D printing, robotics clubs).
Learn simulation tools like MATLAB or ANSYS to test designs digitally.
Understand safety regulations, materials engineering, and quality standards.
Pursue an engineering degree or practical apprenticeship in a chosen discipline.

Healthcare Professional.description: Healthcare Professionals provide medical care, diagnoses, treatment, and counseling to promote physical and mental well-being. They combine science with deep empathy.
learning_path: Focus deeply on biology, chemistry, and anatomy coursework.
Gain experience through volunteering at clinics, hospitals, or nursing homes.
Earn standard CPR, First Aid, or basic lifesaving certifications.
Prepare for and take required professional admission tests (e.g., MCAT, NCLEX).
Develop patient interaction skills and empathy under stress.
Complete clinical rotations or internships under licensed healthcare providers.

Lawyer.description: Lawyers advise clients, represent them in legal proceedings, draft legal documents, and interpret laws. They use logical reasoning, research, and persuasive writing to advocate for justice.
learning_path: Develop excellent reading comprehension, writing, and logical debate skills.
Study history, political science, philosophy, or constitutional law.
Prepare for and take the Law School Admission Test (LSAT).
Obtain a law degree (Juris Doctor or equivalent) from an accredited institution.
Gain experience through mock court trials, debate clubs, or legal internships.
Pass the regional bar examination to become licensed to practice law.

Educator.description: Educators inspire, instruct, and guide learners of various ages. They plan curriculums, explain complex ideas, and facilitate personal and professional growth.
learning_path: Develop public speaking, storytelling, and explanation skills.
Master the subject area you want to teach (e.g., mathematics, languages, art).
Study educational psychology and instructional methodologies.
Practice by tutoring students or volunteer teaching in community programs.
Earn a teaching degree, certification, or corporate training license.
Learn digital teaching tools, learning management systems (LMS), and video tools.

Researcher.description: Researchers investigate scientific, academic, or social phenomena. They run experiments, gather evidence, analyze literature, and publish discoveries to advance human knowledge.
learning_path: Read scientific journals and research papers in your field of interest.
Learn how to formulate hypotheses and design controlled experiments.
Master research statistics and quantitative analysis software (SPSS, R, Python).
Work as a research assistant in a laboratory or academic setting.
Write literature reviews and practice drafting scientific abstracts.
Pursue advanced graduate studies (Master's or Ph.D.) to specialize in research.

Sports Professional.description: Sports Professionals focus on physical fitness, athletic excellence, and coaching. They manage teams, train athletes, study physical education, and promote healthy lifestyles.
learning_path: Maintain a high level of physical fitness and participate in competitive sports.
Study exercise science, kinesiology, nutrition, and sports psychology.
Obtain standard fitness training or coaching credentials (e.g., NASM, ACE).
Gain experience coaching youth leagues, gym clients, or sports camps.
Learn basic first aid, sports medicine, and injury prevention techniques.
Build a network in sports management or sports education fields.
